title: rhaphiolepis indica subsp. umbellata
intro: Rhaphiolepis indica subsp. umbellata, also known as the hawthorn-leaved rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a shrub native to the Himalayan region, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure, heart disease, and digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In traditional herbal medicine, Rhaphiolepis indica subsp. umbellata is prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are also used to support cardiovascular health and boost the immune system.
